days.title: No Time for a Taxicab
days.essence: Walk a turtle on a grid following turn/move instructions and report the Manhattan distance from origin. Part 2 asks for the first coordinate visited twice, requiring a visited-set over every intermediate step rather than just endpoints.

days.title: Bathroom Security
days.essence: Trace a finger across a keypad following U/D/L/R moves, clamping to the pad, to read off a code. Part 2 swaps the square keypad for an irregular diamond shape, so movement must respect arbitrary valid-cell boundaries.

days.title: Squares With Three Sides
days.essence: Count how many triples satisfy the triangle inequality. Part 2 reinterprets the input as columns read in groups of three rows, so the twist is transposing the data before the same check.

days.title: Security Through Obscurity
days.essence: Validate rooms by comparing a checksum against the five most-common letters (ties broken alphabetically), summing valid sector IDs. Part 2 is a Caesar shift by the sector ID to decrypt names and find the target room.

days.title: How About a Nice Game of Chess?
days.essence: Brute-force MD5 hashes of salt+index, collecting hashes with five leading zeros to build an eight-character password. Part 2 uses the sixth hex digit as a position index, requiring care to fill each slot only once and keep scanning a huge index space.

days.title: Signals and Noise
days.essence: Transpose the message lines and take the most-frequent character per column to recover the message. Part 2 simply takes the least-frequent character per column instead.

days.title: Internet Protocol Version 7
days.essence: Split each address into supernet and bracketed hypernet segments, then detect ABBA palindromic patterns for TLS support. Part 2 looks for ABA patterns in supernets that have a corresponding BAB in hypernets, raising the cross-segment matching difficulty.

days.title: Two-Factor Authentication
days.essence: Apply rect/rotate-row/rotate-column operations to a pixel grid and count lit pixels. Part 2 asks you to read the letters drawn on the screen, so the grid must be rendered as ASCII art.

days.title: Explosives in Cyberspace
days.essence: Expand (AxB) markers that repeat the next A characters B times and report the decompressed length. Part 2 makes markers nest, so the expanded length is astronomically large and must be computed recursively by summing sizes without ever materializing the string.

days.title: Balance Bots
days.essence: Model bots passing low/high chips to other bots or output bins, repeatedly firing whichever bot holds two chips. Part 1 finds the bot comparing a specific pair; part 2 multiplies the values that land in three output bins.

days.title: Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generators
days.essence: Find the fewest elevator trips to move all generators and microchips to the top floor without ever frying a chip, a large state-space BFS/A* where the key insight is normalizing states by treating identical chip/generator pairs as interchangeable. Part 2 adds two more elements, exploding the state space and making that symmetry reduction essential.

days.title: Leonardo's Monorail
days.essence: Implement the assembunny instruction set (cpy/inc/dec/jnz) and run it to read register a. Part 2 just initializes register c to 1, leading to far more iterations but the same interpreter.

days.title: A Maze of Twisty Little Cubicles
days.essence: Walls are defined by the parity of the bit count of a coordinate formula, and you BFS the shortest path to a target cell. Part 2 instead counts all cells reachable within 50 steps, a flood-fill over the same implicit maze.

days.title: One-Time Pad
days.essence: A hash is a key if it has a triplet whose character also appears as a quintuple in one of the next 1000 hashes, found via a sliding window of precomputed hashes. Part 2 applies 2016 extra rounds of MD5 stretching per index, hugely increasing cost and rewarding memoization of the rolling window.

days.title: Timing is Everything
days.essence: Find the earliest drop time so a capsule passes through every rotating disc, a system of modular congruences solved by the Chinese Remainder Theorem (or stepped LCM sieving). Part 2 adds one more disc, just another congruence in the system.

days.title: Dragon Checksum
days.essence: Grow data via the dragon-curve rule (a + 0 + reversed-inverted a) until it fills the disk, then repeatedly checksum pairs until the length is odd. Part 2 uses a much larger disk length, so the naive string-building blows up and rewards reasoning about the dragon curve structurally.

days.title: Two Steps Forward
days.essence: Doors in a 4x4 grid open or close based on the MD5 hash of the passcode plus the path taken so far, and you BFS for the shortest path to the vault. Part 2 asks for the length of the longest path that reaches the vault, requiring exhaustive search instead of stopping at the first arrival.

days.title: Like a Rogue
days.essence: Each new row of safe/trap tiles is determined by the three tiles above it (a 1D cellular automaton), and you count safe tiles over 40 rows. Part 2 extends to 400000 rows, so the iteration is heavier but each row is still cheap; cycle detection can short-circuit it.

days.title: An Elephant Named Joseph
days.essence: Elves in a circle steal from a neighbor until one remains, the classic Josephus problem with a closed-form solution based on powers of two. Part 2 changes elimination to the elf directly across the circle, needing a different (power-of-three) recurrence rather than the standard Josephus formula.

days.title: Firewall Rules
days.essence: Given blocked IP ranges, sort and merge the intervals to find the lowest unblocked value. Part 2 counts the total number of allowed addresses across all gaps in the merged ranges.

days.title: Scrambled Letters and Hash
days.essence: Apply a sequence of string operations (swap, rotate, reverse span, move, rotate-by-letter-position) to scramble a password. Part 2 must unscramble a target, inverting each operation, where the position-based rotation is the tricky one to reverse (often brute-forced over all 8! permutations).

days.title: Grid Computing
days.essence: Part 1 counts viable pairs of storage nodes whose data would fit on another. Part 2 is the real puzzle: shuffle data to the goal node by sliding the single empty node around the grid past a wall of oversized nodes, a sliding-block BFS (or hand analysis) where the insight is recognizing it as a 15-puzzle.

days.title: Safe Cracking
days.essence: Extend the assembunny machine with a self-modifying tgl instruction and run it with a=7. Part 2 sets a=12, where naive simulation is far too slow because the loops compute a factorial-plus-constant, so you must disassemble and recognize the multiplication to answer.

days.title: Air Duct Spelunking
days.essence: BFS the grid to get pairwise distances between numbered locations, then solve a Travelling Salesman over those numbers starting at 0. Part 2 requires returning to location 0 at the end, turning the open tour into a closed circuit.

days.title: Clock Signal
days.essence: Add an out instruction to the assembunny machine and find the smallest starting a that produces an infinite alternating 0,1 clock signal. The intended insight is that the program outputs the binary digits of a plus a constant, so you seek the smallest value whose binary form is repeating ...101010.
